p3.title: RP00.P09 micro-phase titles use the generic closeout-pack template names while deliverables are review questions
p3.rationale: RP00.P09.M00-M04 carry micro_titles 'Scope Inventory', 'Contract Draft', 'Type And Shape Definition', 'Primary Implementation Slice', 'Secondary Workflow Slice' while every P09 unit has deliverable_type 'review_question'. This is internally self-consistent and matches the project-wide generic micro-phase scaffold, but the micro_title values cannot be confirmed against docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json from this diff.
p3.recommendation: Confirm these micro_titles match the plan's RP00.P09 micro-phase names during pack creation; no code change needed if they do.

p3.title: Assembler input-guard negative paths are not unit-tested
p3.rationale: assembleControlPlaneHermesValidationEvidenceReviewTransition rejects a non-object/array upstream result and a non-object/array boundaryClaims, but no test exercises those two guards (only object-shaped drift and claim cases are tested).
p3.recommendation: Add two assert.throws cases (null/array upstream result; array boundaryClaims) to lock the guard messages, matching the rigor of the other fail-closed tests.

p3.title: Contract definition duplicates the full result matrices at top level and nested under result
p3.rationale: unit_receipt_matrix, micro_phase_closeout_map, review_question_contracts, and the two contracts appear both at definition top level and inside definition.result, inflating the contract by ~3k lines. This mirrors prior packs and is parity-checked, so it is purely a maintainability/verbosity observation.
p3.recommendation: No action required for this pack; consider whether future packs can reference rather than duplicate the nested result block.

verification_performed.static_parity_checks: 20/20 pass, 0 fail (policy self-validates; counts 61 units / 22 review-question contracts / 10 micro-phase closeouts; range endpoints RP00.P08.M06.S01..RP00.P09.M04.S05)
verification_performed.result_field_parity: policy.resultFields === RESULT_FIELDS const === contract.result_fields (98 fields) — confirmed live
verification_performed.contract_fixture_parity: contract.result deep-equals fixture.result; both pass assertResult; included_subphase_ids identical across policy/fixture/contract/validate-script — confirmed live
verification_performed.assembler_live: assembler consumes the stored CP00-090 fixture result and produces a metadata-only CP00-091 result that deep-equals the stored fixture result (exit 0)
verification_performed.fail_closed_live: verified throws on: upstream pack drift, wrong upstream next_subphase, broken human-approval boundary, write_hermes_evidence_packet, invoke_runtime_claude_review, synthesize_human_approval_decision, bypass_human_approval, unknown claim
verification_performed.evidence_pack: docs/closeout-packs/cp00-091/* intentionally absent — created post-review per scope; not treated as a finding

explicit_checks.permission_boundary: PASS — runtime_permission_evaluation_permitted, permission_engine_execution_permitted, authz_evaluator_called, security_trimming_execution_permitted all false in policy/result/validator; permission_context_visible_only true. Assembler also re-asserts these false on the consumed CP00-090 result.
explicit_checks.audit_boundary: PASS — audit_ledger_append_permitted, audit_event_write_permitted false; audit_context_visible_only true; append_audit_ledger/write_audit_event in forbidden claim set.
explicit_checks.human_approval_boundary: PASS — human_approval_marker_required true, human_approval_marker_is_not_approval true, human_approval_record_write_permitted/decision_synthesized/bypass_permitted all false; synthesize/bypass/create-runtime-approval/write-record forbidden and live-rejected. Upstream CP00-090 marker is consumed (not re-synthesized).
explicit_checks.claude_runtime_boundary: PASS — runtime_claude_review_invocation_permitted false; every review_question_contract carries runtime_review_invocation_permitted false; invoke_runtime_claude_review forbidden and live-rejected. Claude Review Packet bound as metadata only.
explicit_checks.write_boundaries: PASS — service_logic, route, ui, external asset, error/retry/rollback/compensation/incident, observability, hermes_evidence_packet_write, command_evidence_write, review_queue/assignment/notification, database, storage, product_state, idempotency, lock all false; writes_product_state false; no_real_data true.
explicit_checks.cp00_plan_binding: PASS — packId/plannedPackId CP00-091, requestedPackId CP00-090 with explicit live-cursor correctionReason; planRef docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json; Risk C; sourceMicroPhaseId RP00.P09.M04; completedPhaseIds [RP00.P08]; openSourceMicroPhaseIds [RP00.P09.M05]; nextSubphase RP00.P09.M05.S01. Upstream chain enforced: assembler requires upstream pack_id/planned_pack_id CP00-090 and upstream next_subphase RP00.P08.M06.S01, then runs full assertControlPlaneHermesValidationHumanApprovalMarkerResult.
explicit_checks.validator_coverage: PASS — assertPolicy checks 23 scalar fields, phase/completed-phase/subphase arrays, requiredInputFields==CP00-090 fields, resultFields length lock, source map, matrix lengths (61/10/22), per-row metadata-only invariants, ~50 boolean invariants, and closeout flags. assertResult enforces exact key-set (no extra/missing fields), ~60 fixed values, trimmed-string fields, deep array parity to policy, empty blocked_claim_refs, and contract-object parity. validate-rp00 script adds the 61 ids to requiredClosedSubphases, advances currentContractSubphaseId/currentSourceMicroPhaseId, maps construction-inspection subphase to the terminal id, and requires control_plane_hermes_validation_evidence_review_transition_verified.
explicit_checks.fixture_contract_parity: PASS — contract.result === fixture.result (deep); contract.required_input_fields/result_fields/included_subphase_ids/unit_titles/source_micro_phase_ids/phase_ids/required_evidence_refs/acceptance_gate_ids/forbidden_boundary_suite_claims/fail_closed_on/unit_receipt_matrix/micro_phase_closeout_map/review_question_contracts all asserted against policy by the new contract test; both result copies pass assertResult.
explicit_checks.missing_tests: Minor gaps only (P3). Positive assembler path, 6 fail-closed paths, module shape, fixture parity, and contract parity are all covered.
